defends: The delivered Next.js app sets the security response headers (CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy) that harden the browser against drive-by compromise and injected script (OWASP A05).
self_guard: bad fixture config is missing >=1 required header (negative control fires as fail); good fixture sets all five and passes clean.
note: Fixed evidence manifest — what the security-headers check audits is not model discretion (WORKING_METHOD §1). This verifies the required header NAMES are configured in the app's static header config; it does not evaluate each header's VALUE/strength (e.g. CSP directive tightness) — that is policy review, not a mechanical presence check.
